title: tomato provider
description: Native OpenTofu/Terraform provider for Tomato-firmware routers (FreshTomato / AdvancedTomato) driven over SSH (Dropbear). Manages NVRAM generically (tomato_nvram resource + data source) with a manage-declared-only subset model so declared keys import to 0-diff and unmanaged NVRAM is never touched. Tomato has no clean REST API; SSH chosen over HTTP/update.cgi because the HTTP read path is unworkable for a generic resource.

phases.title: Core (generic NVRAM over SSH)
phases.exit_criteria: Builds, vets, tests; one resource + data source manage any NVRAM variable to 0-diff.

items.feature: SSH transport over the system ssh binary (os/exec, BatchMode, ConnectTimeout): nvram get/set/unset/commit, service restart, nvram show; unset-vs-set-empty disambiguation; shell-quoting. No new Go deps.
tomato_nvram resource (CRUD + ImportState): set declared keys -> commit -> restart; snapshot prior values; restore-on-destroy (set-or-unset); add/drop keys handled in Update.
Manage-declared-only drift: nvramSubsetMatches plan modifier (declared keys match device -> 0-diff; unmanaged NVRAM never clobbered).
tomato_nvram data source (read single key value+present, or whole `nvram show`).
Unit tests: subset matcher, key-map/snapshot encode/decode, snapEntry, keyID, splitKeys, splitHostPort, shellQuote, connectTimeout.

phases.title: Validate against live Tomato
phases.exit_criteria: A lab Tomato imports to 0-diff through the provider.

items.feature: make check green (gofmt+vet+test+build); pushed to main; pre-commit gate passes.

Live import to 0-diff against a lab Tomato. BLOCKED: only NetBox Tomato is tomato64-lab (device 24, VM 92007 / 100.64.92.7) — powered-off placeholder, 'Not a semaphore-target', no primary IP, all ports closed. Powering it on is change-windowed; out of scope. Skipped.
Acceptance tests (TF_ACC) against a throwaway/lab Tomato.

phases.title: Ergonomics + release
phases.exit_criteria: Optional typed sugar; CI; registry publish.

items.feature: Typed sugar resources (lan, wan, dhcp, wifi) over the generic tomato_nvram core, if an ergonomics need emerges.
CI (build/vet/test) + GoReleaser + registry publish + docs gen.
